name: Tabs
description: Inline mutually-exclusive selector. Visual chrome delegated to Filter chip — each segment renders with `chorus-chip chorus-chip--filter` classes and inherits the chip's container/label/border/state recipes verbatim. This sub adds only the row container (transparent, horizontal padding, fixed inter-segment gap) and the single-select semantics enforced via Tabs context.

onChange.description: Fired with the next value when a segment is selected.

label.description: Same contract as Filter chip's label slot.

leadingIcon.description: Same contract as Filter chip's leadingIcon slot.

selectionStates.note: Each segment swaps between Filter chip's unselected (surfaceContainerHigh + outlineVariant border) and selected (inverseSurface + inverseOnSurface) recipes.

states.note: Hover / pressed / focus overlays and the disabled treatment are inherited from Filter chip; same opacities, same focus ring composition.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the segment is in. Re-anchored inside the segment from Filter chip's default outward composition because the Segmented row is a horizontal scroller.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: The Tabs row is a horizontal scroller; an outward ring would clip at the row's top/bottom edges.

ring.outerLayerPosition: depth 0..2px from the segment edge (the outer stroke)
ring.insetLayerPosition: depth 2..3px from the segment edge (the counter-ring just inside the outer stroke)
ring.implementation: inset box-shadow on the segment's `::after` overlay. Constrained strictly inside the segment's footprint and never exceeds it.

focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)
